cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Checkout is returning 500 errors. Multiple systems may be involved. Investigate before acting.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: INCIDENT PROTOCOL: Always investigate before remediating. Check downstream dependencies first. Do not restart services blindly â€” identify the root cause.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Redis cache failure causing payment service timeouts and checkout 500 errors

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: A canary deploy triggered alerts across monitoring. Latency is spiking but the cause is unclear. Investigate the deployment pipeline and downstream effects.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: DEPLOY PROTOCOL: Never fully rollback without investigating. Check canary logs, then metrics, then pod health. Feature flags may mask issues.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Bad canary deployment with memory leak causing latency spikes and config mismatch

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: A network partition has split the cluster. Some nodes are unreachable but the scope of damage is unclear. Diagnose before reconnecting.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: PARTITION PROTOCOL: 1) Scan both zones. 2) Check replication lag â€” if data diverged, do NOT force reconnect. 3) Verify quorum before electing leader. 4) Heal partition only after full diagnosis.

check_replication_lag.condition: discovered.scanned_zone_a == true AND discovered.scanned_zone_b == true AND discovered.checked_replication == false

cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Network partition between regions causing cross-region communication failure and data sync loss

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Users report missing files. The storage layer may be corrupted but the extent is unknown. Investigate carefully â€” wrong actions could cause data loss.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: DATA INCIDENT PROTOCOL: 1) IMMEDIATELY set read-only mode to prevent further writes. 2) Check WAL before anything. 3) Map corruption extent. 4) Never restore from backup without verifying replica first.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Primary disk corruption causing replication failure and risk of data loss across storage nodes

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Anomalous login patterns detected. A possible credential breach, but scope is unclear. Investigate the chain: entry point, lateral movement, data access.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: SECURITY INCIDENT RESPONSE: 1) Analyze login anomalies to identify compromised accounts. 2) Trace lateral movement. 3) Check data access logs. 4) Lock compromised accounts. 5) Rotate exposed API keys. Do NOT rotate keys before identifying which are compromised.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Credential breach via exposed API key causing unauthorized access and requiring emergency lockdown

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Core network switch failure detected. Database connections are timing out and API errors are spiking. Multiple domains affected â€” coordinate investigation across network, database, and application layers.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: NETWORK INCIDENT: 1) Diagnose core switch before failover. 2) Check BGP sessions for route flapping. 3) After switch is stable, restart DB connection pools. 4) Only restart API pods after DB connections are healthy. Blind restarts will cause connection storms.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: BGP route failure causing CDN outage and cascading routing failures across edge nodes

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Database lock contention is escalating. API response times are through the roof and payment processing is failing. Revenue is dropping every minute this continues.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: DB OVERLOAD PROTOCOL: 1) Analyze slow queries to identify the bottleneck. 2) Kill long-running queries blocking others. 3) Clear lock contention. 4) Enable application circuit breakers to reduce DB pressure. 5) Only optimize indexes after load is reduced. Do NOT scale infrastructure before clearing the lock storm.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Runaway query storm causing database connection pool exhaustion and cascading service timeouts

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Application pods are crash-looping due to OOM kills. Each restart creates a database connection storm. The cascading restarts are overwhelming the container runtime.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: OOM CASCADE PROTOCOL: 1) Check app logs for memory leak source. 2) Scale DOWN instances first to stop the restart storm. 3) Set memory limits. 4) Kill idle DB connections to relieve the storm. 5) Enable connection pooler. 6) Only then rollback deployment. Do NOT restart kubelet while connection storm is active.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Application out-of-memory crash causing restart loops and cascading dependency failures

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Primary region database has failed. Replication to secondary region is broken. Application is partially serving stale data. Risk of data loss if failover is mishandled.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: MULTI-REGION FAILOVER: 1) Diagnose primary failure cause. 2) Check replication status and data divergence. 3) Check WAL for uncommitted transactions. 4) Only promote secondary after confirming divergence is manageable. 5) Switch traffic AFTER promotion. Premature traffic switch causes split-brain.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Primary region failure requiring emergency failover to secondary region with data consistency verification

cascade_tasks_dataset.description: Database deadlock chain is escalating. Payment gateway is timing out, checkout success rate has dropped to near zero, and revenue impact is growing every second. Conflicting signals: infra metrics look normal but application layer is broken.
cascade_tasks_dataset.playbook_text: DEADLOCK CASCADE PROTOCOL: 1) Analyze the deadlock graph to find the root blocker. 2) Kill the blocking transaction. 3) Enable payment retry to recover in-flight payments. 4) Rebuild indexes only after deadlocks are cleared. 5) WARNING: infra appears healthy â€” do NOT chase false signals at the infrastructure layer.
cascade_tasks_dataset.root_cause: Database lock contention from bad deployment query causing transaction failures and direct revenue loss
